questions.question: Your Piper Cub is not equipped with a VSI, you want to know what your climb rate is. You time yourself and see that every 18 seconds you climb 100'. What is your climb rate in meters per second?
How long will it take to climb to cruise altitude?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 11-15: Joey plans a flight from KMRN (1270') to KTYS (979') at 8,500' MSL on a true course of 277°. The total distance is 118 NM and he will cruise at 145 MPH TAS and 8.7 GPH with winds aloft 240° at 14 KTS. His average climb performance will be 650 FPM at 92 KTAS and 9.8 GPH with average winds of 215° at 15 KTS. His average descent performance will be 900 FPM at 110 KTAS and 5.2 GPH with average winds of 320° at 8 KTS.

questions.question: You and your friends are practicing slow flight in the twin when an engine fails. You know from your performance charts that at your current weight and atmospheric conditions you should be able to climb at 400 FPM on one engine. However, that is in a clean configuration. Right now the gear is down (reduction of 35% in climb rate), the flaps are at 30° (reduction of 28% in climb rate), and the propeller of the bad engine is not feathered (reduction of 12% in climb rate). What is your climb rate before you clean up the aircraft?

choices.text: No rate of climb; you're descending at 100 FPM

questions.question: After that takeoff, the average rate of climb is 1680 fpm for 200 feet and 1120 fpm for the rest of the climb. How long will it take to climb from the airport elevation of 985 feet to 3500 feet MSL?
An F-16 is maintaining 200 knots true airspeed in a straight vertical climb. What rate is it climbing at in feet per second?

The fighter has an average speed of 582 knots and has to travel 304 nautical miles to his intercept point. He'll climb at 5,800 feet per minute from sea level to 17,000 feet. How long will that climb take?
questions.context: In the preflight briefing for your upcoming 328 statute mile cross country trip you learn that there will be a TFR directly under your true course of 332 degrees. The center of the TFR will be located 185 nautical miles along your route. The TFR is 5 nautical miles across, from the surface to 7,000 feet, and will be active from 1400Z to 1600Z. Use this information to answer the next 7 questions.

questions.question: If an aircraft departs an airport with an elevation of 715 feet and climbs to 5500 feet with an average rate of climb of 515 fpm, how long will it take to climb?
You depart an airport with an elevation of 1023'. At an average rate of climb of 678 ft./min., how long will it take to climb to an altitude of 9,500' at a groundspeed of 94 kts?

With that question answered, you now have time to work on your first problem. The good news is that you started your stop watch exactly 3 minutes and 48 seconds ago as you passed through 1200 feet. Since your climb rate is 520 feet per minute, what is your altitude now- a. Enter your answer on the answer sheet
At a climb rate of 2400 feet per minute and a ground speed of 105 knots, how many nautical miles will you cover during 5 minutes of climb?

If an aircraft departs an airport with a field elevation of 2,215 feet, and climbs to a cruise altitude of 8,433 feet with an average climb rate of 612 feet per minute, how long will it take to reach the cruise altitude?

You are flying at an altitude of 5,500 ft MSL. Ahead of you is a mountain with its peak at 8,000 ft MSL. Wind is 230° true at 27 kts, with a calibrated airspeed of 130 kts. The temperature and pressure are standard, and your magnetic course is 286°, with a magnetic variation of 7° east. How many nautical miles away do you need to begin your climb to clear the peak by 500 ft if your climb rate will be a steady 400 ft/min?
If it takes five minutes to climb to 7400 ft MSL from sea level and if the same rate of climb is sustained, how long will it take to climb from sea level to 18,000 ft MSL?

A Cessna 210 climbs from an airport (250 ft MSL) to 5000 ft MSL in 4:30. Then, it takes 5:20 to climb from 5000 ft MSL to 10,000 ft MSL. A finally, it takes 6:40 to climb from 10,000 ft MSL to 15,000 MSL. What is the average rate of climb the Cessna 210 attains in the climb from the airport to 15,000 MSL?
